keyPoints: Friction budget: daily cap on AI prompts paired with nudges toward real-world social engagement.
Three-arm 12-week randomized design: control unrestricted AI use; FB; FB+HP.
Primary outcomes: Belonging and Well-being; secondary outcomes: loneliness, real-world socialization, AI dependence.
Hypotheses: reduced AI duration mediates increased human contact and belonging; bounded, personal AI interactions outperform simple caps.
Measurement plan: baseline, weekly AI-use logs, mid-point, end, optional follow-up; validated scales; privacy safeguards; preregistration.
Design considerations: safety nets, heterogeneity, adaptive budgets; actionable design guidelines for practitioners.
